reasoning: This question asks whether the 10-year TIPS yield on a future date will have increased compared to its value on 2025-10-26. Assuming a near-random walk or mean-reversion behavior over the short to medium term for long-term yields, the probability of an increase vs an exact decrease or unchanged value is roughly a coin flip, slightly tilted due to current macro trends. Given the lack of clear directional macroeconomic consensus about late 2025 to early 2026 interest rates and a symmetric random walk baseline for yields, I assign a probability of 0.48, allowing for slight structural mean reversion downwards if rates are currently historically high.
